## Overview

VE3 Global Ltd is the designated aggregator for the UK Government's Fuel Finder open data scheme, operating the central platform under a GBP 2M contract with the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) signed on 3 May 2025. VE3 Global operates the Fuel Finder service at fuel-finder.service.gov.uk, handling retailer registration, fuel price data submission, and the Public API for data consumers. The company had no prior experience in the fuel sector when awarded the contract. The platform went live on 2 February 2026.

## Products & Services

| Product | Category | Relevance to Project |
|---------|----------|---------------------|
| Fuel Finder Public API | Open Data API | Primary data source for CMA service -- read-only access to fuel prices and forecourt data |
| Fuel Finder Price Submission API | Data Ingestion | Retailer price submission (not consumed by CMA directly) |
| Fuel Finder Portal | Web Application | Retailer registration and manual price submission via GOV.UK One Login |
| CSV Bulk Download | Data Export | Twice-daily CSV export of current prices and forecourt details |
| Developer Portal | Documentation | API documentation, developer guidelines, sandbox environment |

## Pricing Model

- API access: Free (open data under OGL v3.0)
- CSV download: Free
- No per-request charges
- Funded by DESNZ contract (GBP 2M)

## UK Government Presence

- G-Cloud listed: Unknown
- DOS listed: Unknown
- UK data centres: Yes (GOV.UK service infrastructure)
- Digital Marketplace contract: DESNZ direct award (May 2025)

## Compliance and Security

| Standard | Status |
|----------|--------|
| UK Data Residency | Yes -- GOV.UK infrastructure |
| GOV.UK One Login Integration | Yes -- retailer authentication |
| OAuth 2.0 | Yes -- API authentication |
| OGL v3.0 | Yes -- data licensing |
| HTTPS/TLS 1.2+ | Yes -- mandatory for all API access |

## Strengths

- Sole provider of statutory fuel price data for all ~8,500 UK forecourts
- Standard REST API with OAuth 2.0 authentication
- Published developer guidelines with caching, rate limiting, and security recommendations
- Separate test and production environments
- Incremental sync capability via effective-start-timestamp
- Multiple data access channels (API, CSV, email subscription)

## Weaknesses

- No prior experience in the fuel sector when awarded contract
- Significant data quality issues post-launch: prices displayed as 1.3p/litre (unit confusion), 500+ forecourts with incorrect location data, implausible prices (GBP 15/litre)
- Registration opened 2 weeks behind schedule
- Unannounced backend IT changes affecting retailers without consultation
- GBP 2M contract reportedly at risk due to persistent technical issues
- API documentation hosted in React SPA (not downloadable OpenAPI spec)
- Conservative rate limit (30 RPM) with sequential processing constraint
- No webhook/push notification for price changes (polling required)

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Notes |
|------|-----------|--------|-------|
| Platform outage | MEDIUM | HIGH | Documented technical issues since launch |
| Data quality errors | HIGH | HIGH | Multiple documented incidents (Feb-Mar 2026) |
| Contract termination | LOW-MEDIUM | CRITICAL | Government contingency planning reported |
| API surface change | LOW | MEDIUM | New operator may redesign API |
